caption: Got a cancellation Thursday morning. Thinking of taking Elise and Beau out if you can get them to Bayport by 5:30. Tide's good early.
They'd love that. Ashley gets off at 4 so that works. What should they bring?
PFDs I got. Just sunscreen and snacks. Tell Beau no sneakers on the deck — Elise already knows the drill.
Ha. He'll fight you on that. Any fish this week or has it been slow?
Tuesday was solid — red snapper about 8 miles out. Six clients, came home happy. Wednesday rained us out.
Nice. You taking Lucky Strike or the small skiff?
Lucky Strike. More shade for the kids. Might let Elise run the throttle in the channel if she wants.
She'll never let us forget it. See you Thursday.

They'd love that. Ashley gets off at 4 so that works. What should they bring?

They'd love that. Ashley gets off at 4 so that works. What should they bring?

Sunrise over Bayport harbor. Diesels warming.
1963 Martin D-28 — bridge plate day. Original stamping intact.
Fresh grouper on the cast iron. Miller's Smokehouse in the background.
Grand-kids on the flats. Beau finally got his first redfish.
Workshop bench, Hank Williams on the turntable, coffee still hot.
'72 Telecaster back on the wall. Fret leveling and setup complete.
Ledge 12 grouper bite this morning. Tip pool was kind.
Sunday at Grace Community. Diane brought the coleslaw.
Off-season winch rebuild finished. Ready for April.
Cummins 6BT injector service. Three rebuilds and still smooth.
Toys-for-Tots benefit jam poster — 12/5 at Grace hall.
Fall Music Festival — Harbor Ramblers on the two-song set.
Refret complete on the '68 J-45. Client picking up Saturday.
Fish fry at American Legion Post 99 for Veterans Day.
Diane's birthday dinner at Riverside — Marcel comped dessert.
Marcel plated the special: shrimp and grits. Chef's kid at work.
Biscuit on the porch, evening light. All quiet.
New pickups from Etsy — hand-wound, sound like the '60s.
Neck reset alignment jig — mine's been on the bench 20 years.
Weeki Wachee mermaid trip with Elise and Beau.
Bayport marina dock walk — front edge holding offshore.
'57 Gibson ES-125 in for full setup. Beautiful player.
Christmas Eve at Luc and Ashley's. Grand-kids everywhere.
Thanksgiving turkey — Marcel's citrus brine at the cottage.
Cast iron ribs — smoked eight hours, dry-rubbed.
Charter wind-down dinner at Nolan's. Season's done.
USCG renewal packet mailed. Now we wait 45 days.
Bass at the North edge — nine keepers by 10 AM.
Repair intake for the '78 P-Bass. Original hardware intact.
Bringing home a '75 Rickenbacker 4001 for restoration.
Chest freezer full of snapper and grouper. Winter's covered.
Grouper season peak — three trips this week alone.
Fresh smoked pork ribs on the smoker. Diane made slaw.
Sunday afternoon flats — Elise on the bow with her Ugly Stik.
Fret polish on the '65 SG. Original PAFs still singing.
Nolan brought over a '71 Les Paul Deluxe. Refret time.
Deck wash and stow. End of a clean charter.
Bayport Herald weekly on the porch. Coffee's strong.
Sunday roast at Diane's. Grand-kids and Biscuit under the table.
'96 Martin HD-28 setup done — action low, intonation dialed.
First cold front of the season. Fish moved shallower.
Charter with the Hollis group — Ellis brought his Taylor along.
Sunset run back into Bayport. Lucky Strike putting down miles.
Marcel's citrus-brined grouper — best of the season.
Refret on the '85 Strat. New wire, feels reborn.
Ice chest, cold beer, cooler of snapper. Charter done.
Ashley made a lemon pie for Sunday. Elise had two slices.
Grace Community — pastor Chambers on Ecclesiastes today.
Wide-open water day — Padgett group filled the box.
Charter log entry for October — pipeline coming down.
Pastor Chambers dropping off his Gibson for a fret job.
Full moon over the dock — Bayport at its best.
1968 J-45 back home. Client cried, in a good way.
Chili slow-cooked from scratch. Two hours to go.
Old B.B. King poster still on the wall. 22 years and counting.
Repair pickup handoff — hardest part is letting them go home.
Rebuilt outrigger winch, five years of hard use.
Vintage Kay archtop off the shelf — bracing looking rough.
Diane sent this from the tailgate — Gators up two touchdowns.
Christmas lights on the palm tree. Cottage looks like December.
'99 D-28 refret complete. Nickel-silver, medium jumbos.
Half-day charter with the Ashworth party — good crew.
Cummins port oil sample looks clean at 400 hours.
Grand-kids brought a school project home. Elise drew the boat.
Marcel back at Riverside after a two-day trip. Kitchen looks tight.
Freshly cut tonewood at LMI — smells like a new project.
'62 B-25 refret and setup ready. Original tuners spared.
Bayport Bait & Tackle running low on live shrimp again.
Sunset at the dock. Diesels off, coffee empty.
Repair board full through December. Off-season pipeline holding.
Diane's birthday flowers on the workbench. She'll never look here.
Nolan and I ran the same reef today — 15 min apart, hers full first.
Snapper limit at buoy 12 by 8 AM. Some days you get gifts.
Rita brought her studio Strat for a full setup. Beautiful axe.
Field-strip on the '78 Precision Bass — pickup pole spacing was off.
Salt-air check on the tools. Snap-Ons still hold up 40 years in.
Charter with the Whitmer party — soft chop, wide open bite.
Elise reading on the dock while I cleaned the reels. Perfect Sunday.
Repair status board updated for October.
'80 Guild D-25 back on the wall — bridge reglue and setup done.
Cummins stbd fuel filter secondary — dark. Swapped both.
Repair intake day — three instruments through the shop.
Marcel and I split a plate of ribs on the porch. Rare quiet night.
Bayport Herald ran a story on the Fall Music Festival.
Coming home with the box heavy — grouper day.
Repair pickup for Miguel from Miami — long haul but worth it.
Hank Williams album finally back on the platter after the needle swap.
Grace Community 8:30 — Diane singing alto today.
